name: project-operator
description: Operates and debugs the local stack (API/worker/frontend); focuses on observability, logs, and safe automation.
capabilities: Diagnose issues across API, worker, and frontend components
Tail and analyze logs from multiple services
Check service status and health endpoints safely
Apply incremental changes with revert strategies
Provide stop/cancel strategies for long-running commands
Explain configuration changes in plain language
author: ACSKamloops
securitySummary: This is a prompt-only documentation skill with no executable code. The SKILL.md provides operational guidance for debugging local development environments. The static analysis findings are false positives triggered by SHA256 hashes and version strings being misidentified as cryptographic patterns. No network calls, filesystem access, or external commands exist.
valueStatement: Debugging distributed local stacks can be chaotic with unclear error sources and risky restarts. This skill provides structured observability practices, log-focused debugging, and safe automation patterns for API, worker, and frontend components.
limitations: Does not execute code or restart services automatically
Does not access remote production environments
Does not modify system files or configurations outside the project

useCases.title: Debug distributed systems
useCases.description: Trace errors across API, worker, and frontend layers using structured log analysis and health checks.

useCases.title: Monitor local services
useCases.description: Check service status, tail logs, and apply changes safely with clear rollback procedures.

useCases.title: Reproduce and isolate issues
useCases.description: Follow incremental debugging steps and use observability tools to isolate root causes.

promptTemplates.title: Check service health
promptTemplates.scenario: Verify all stack components are running
promptTemplates.prompt: Check the health of all services in my local stack. Identify any that are down or showing errors. Report status for each component.

promptTemplates.title: Tail and analyze logs
promptTemplates.prompt: Tail the logs for my local stack. Look for error patterns and identify the most recent issues across all services.

promptTemplates.title: Safe configuration change
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to update my configuration. Explain the impact of the change and provide a revert path in case something goes wrong.

promptTemplates.title: Debug long-running operation
promptTemplates.prompt: I started a long-running command and it seems stuck. Check the status and provide a strategy to stop or cancel it safely.

bestPractices: Read logs before restarting any service to understand the root cause
Make incremental changes so each change is observable and can be reverted
Always ask for a revert strategy before applying configuration changes
antiPatterns: Restarting services without first reading logs or checking error messages
Making large, sweeping changes without a rollback plan
Running long commands without knowing how to stop them

faq.question: Does this skill restart services automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill focuses on observability and safe automation patterns. It will advise on restart strategies but never execute them without explicit instruction.

faq.question: Can I use this with production environments?
faq.answer: This skill is designed for local development stacks. Production debugging requires different safety protocols and access controls.

faq.question: What stack components are supported?
faq.answer: The skill works with API, worker, and frontend components. It focuses on reading logs, checking status, and providing guidance.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This is a prompt-only skill. It reads logs and status information from your local stack but does not transmit data externally.

faq.question: Why are my services not starting?
faq.answer: Check the logs first. Common issues include port conflicts, missing environment variables, or configuration errors. The skill can help analyze logs.

faq.question: How is this different from general debugging tools?
faq.answer: This skill provides structured debugging workflows specifically for distributed local stacks with emphasis on safe, observable changes.
